chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here I have written those classes of functions in the increasing order of their weightage.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This means that n is always less than n log n and that is always less than n square or

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: log n is always less than root n as well as n as well as so it is less than all of them.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So n power 3 then goes on n power 4, 5 or up to 100 also that is less than 2 power n

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and 2 power n is less than 3 power n so that is how they are written in the increasing

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: order of their weightage.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now to prove that I will take few terms from this one, few type classes from this one.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us say log n n, n square 2 power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us try this.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take n value as 1, log 1 will be 0 and this will be 1 and this will be 2.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take this as 2, then log 2 base 2 is 1, this is 1 and this is 4 and this is also

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take n value as 8, then this is 3 and if I take n value as 4, then this will be

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 16 and 2 power 4 is also 16 and this is 2.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take n value as 8, this will be 3 and this will be 64 and this is 2 power 8 that

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So you can see that there is a sudden change.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See I have taken 8 here so there is a slower growth and there is a very faster growth in

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the value.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is 2 power n and you can see that now if you go on putting the values, this

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is less than this one and this is less than this one, this is less than this one.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you observe this, up to here, these two values were equal and here also these two

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: values were equal and this time it was greater.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Again it is greater.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So sometime it was less or greater or equal whatever it may be but from this point onwards

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we can guarantee that 2 power n will be moving with a faster speed.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if I take 9 here, then some log of 9 may be I may be getting 3.1 something but here

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 81 and this will be 512.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 2 power 9 is 512.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So around 30 values, around 16 or 15 is added here so that much increase but there you can

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: see that there is a bigger change in the value.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is doubled again.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So always 2 power n is greater and above that is greater than that is 3 power n and the

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: highest is 2 power n power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So from this we can say that for some values of n, this is smaller than this one or this

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is greater than this one.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So they are arranged in the order.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So even if you have n power 100, that is also less than 2 power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You write anything to the power of n, power k.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: k can be anything the value that you can imagine, you can write on that and that is

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: going to be less than 2 power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How come?

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See n means don't assume the values of n very small that is starting from 1 or 2 or 10.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Usually n means we imagine it as 10 or 5 like that.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No, n can be any number.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So n can be 0 to infinity.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for larger values of n at some point n power 100 will be smaller than 2 power n and

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: from that point onwards 2 power n will be always greater.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there may be some starting value of n for which that will be greater.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: On the graph for n, suppose this is log n, this line is showing n and this line is showing

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n square.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose this is log n and this is n, this is showing n square and this is showing n

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: cube then this will show 2 power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This represents 2 power n.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So initially the value of 2 power n may be smaller than suddenly it will have a higher

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: rate of increase.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is not for same x and y values.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So just n values I have written and I have drawn some lines here.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So representing this is smaller than this one so this is smaller than this so this is

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: greater than this.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So like that 2 power n is greater.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So initially it may be smaller for some values of n then suddenly it may increase.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this inequality is very very important.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is very important.

chunks.title: Compare Class of Functions - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The order in which they are arranged that is very important.

text: Here I have written those classes of functions in the increasing order of their weightage. This means that n is always less than n log n and that is always less than n square or log n is always less than root n as well as n as well as so it is less than all of them. So n power 3 then goes on n power 4, 5 or up to 100 also that is less than 2 power n and 2 power n is less than 3 power n so that is how they are written in the increasing order of their weightage. Now to prove that I will take few terms from this one, few type classes from this one. Let us say log n n, n square 2 power n. Let us try this. If I take n value as 1, log 1 will be 0 and this will be 1 and this will be 2. If I take this as 2, then log 2 base 2 is 1, this is 1 and this is 4 and this is also 4. If I take n value as 8, then this is 3 and if I take n value as 4, then this will be 16 and 2 power 4 is also 16 and this is 2. If I take n value as 8, this will be 3 and this will be 64 and this is 2 power 8 that is 256. So you can see that there is a sudden change. See I have taken 8 here so there is a slower growth and there is a very faster growth in the value. So that is 2 power n and you can see that now if you go on putting the values, this is less than this one and this is less than this one, this is less than this one. If you observe this, up to here, these two values were equal and here also these two values were equal and this time it was greater. Again it is greater. So sometime it was less or greater or equal whatever it may be but from this point onwards we can guarantee that 2 power n will be moving with a faster speed. So if I take 9 here, then some log of 9 may be I may be getting 3.1 something but here 81 and this will be 512. 2 power 9 is 512. So around 30 values, around 16 or 15 is added here so that much increase but there you can see that there is a bigger change in the value. It is doubled again. So always 2 power n is greater and above that is greater than that is 3 power n and the highest is 2 power n power n. So from this we can say that for some values of n, this is smaller than this one or this is greater than this one. So they are arranged in the order. So even if you have n power 100, that is also less than 2 power n. You write anything to the power of n, power k. k can be anything the value that you can imagine, you can write on that and that is going to be less than 2 power n. How come? See n means don't assume the values of n very small that is starting from 1 or 2 or 10. Usually n means we imagine it as 10 or 5 like that. No, n can be any number. So n can be 0 to infinity. So for larger values of n at some point n power 100 will be smaller than 2 power n and from that point onwards 2 power n will be always greater. So there may be some starting value of n for which that will be greater. On the graph for n, suppose this is log n, this line is showing n and this line is showing n square. Suppose this is log n and this is n, this is showing n square and this is showing n cube then this will show 2 power n. This represents 2 power n. So initially the value of 2 power n may be smaller than suddenly it will have a higher rate of increase. So this is not for same x and y values. So just n values I have written and I have drawn some lines here. So representing this is smaller than this one so this is smaller than this so this is greater than this. So like that 2 power n is greater. So initially it may be smaller for some values of n then suddenly it may increase. So this inequality is very very important. This is very important. The order in which they are arranged that is very important.
